# UX Data Performance API Security Checklist: View Forecast Accuracy and Compare Predictions to Actuals

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of requirements across UX, data, performance, API, and security concerns for UC-14 before implementation work begins.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/sahmed/Documents/311-forecast-system/specs/014-uc-14-forecast-accuracy/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the planner-facing analysis needs defined beyond loading the default view, including what must be visible for rendered-with-metrics, rendered-without-metrics, unavailable, and error outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Data Model §View Status]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements defined for how UC-14 resolves retained daily forecast history for the requested scope without introducing unsupported product-selection branches? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyRequest]
- [x] CHK003 Are the required attributes of a forecast-performance request fully specified, including default last-30-completed-days scope, category and geography filters, comparison granularity, and correlation context? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyRequest]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for all API consumers involved in the flow, including the authenticated planner retrieving comparison data and the authenticated client reporting final render outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-018, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK005 Does the specification define what performance expectations apply to non-success outcomes such as metric fallback, empty overlap, missing forecasts, missing actuals, and client render failures? [Gap, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Performance Goals, Data Model §Request Status]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “clear accuracy metrics” translated into observable UI and data requirements rather than left as an outcome-only statement about trust or interpretability? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK007 Is the default window of “last 30 completed days” unambiguous about timezone handling, exclusion of the current partial day, and how explicit user-selected ranges override it? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK008 Is “corresponding actual demand for the same periods and scope” precise about bucket boundaries, category and geography scope matching, and partial-overlap handling? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-019, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK009 Is “attempt on-demand computation” clear enough to distinguish reusable retained UC-06 metrics from request-scoped fallback computation and failure semantics? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Service Direction, Data Model §Metric Resolution Status]
- [x] CHK010 Is the distinction between server-side prepared comparison output and client-side final render reporting clear enough to avoid conflicting interpretations of “render succeeds” versus “response prepared successfully”? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-017, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyRenderEvent]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are default-load requirements consistent between the user stories, edge cases, functional requirements, and implementation note that the current partial day must be excluded? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK012 Are metric-availability rules consistent between the spec’s fallback wording and the data model distinction among `retrieved_precomputed`, `computed_on_demand`, `unavailable`, and `failed`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005 through §FR-006, Spec §FR-014 through §FR-015, Data Model §Metric Resolution Status]
- [x] CHK013 Are unavailable and error-state requirements consistent with the plan’s request-status vocabulary and the contract’s `viewStatus` response semantics? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012 through §FR-017, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §View Status, Contract §components.schemas.ViewStatus]
- [x] CHK014 Are category-only and category-plus-geography comparison requirements consistent with optional geography fields across the spec, data model, and API response schema? [Consistency, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Spec §Key Entities, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyView, Contract §components.schemas.ForecastAccuracyResponse]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the success criteria objectively verify planner-facing UX value, or do they mainly measure backend retrieval and alignment without enough evidence of interpretability in the rendered experience? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK016 Are performance-related outcomes measurable for each critical path, including default view retrieval, metric fallback, unavailable-state responses, and render-event acceptance, rather than only successful comparisons? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001 through §SC-005, Plan §Performance Goals, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK017 Can the requirement that request outcomes remain traceable be objectively verified without a more explicit definition of required request, actor, and correlation identifiers across server and client events? [Measurability, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-018, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyRequest, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyRenderEvent]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary UX scenarios defined for the default last-30-days load, scoped analysis changes, metrics-available rendering, metrics-unavailable rendering, unavailable results, and explicit error states? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK019 Are alternate data scenarios specified for requests that remain on retained daily forecast history while still allowing scope-driven granularity changes in the UI or API? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyRequest]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery requirements defined for what should happen after a client render failure is reported, or is the lifecycle intentionally limited to observability after the prepared result has been stored? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Spec §FR-017 through §FR-018, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyRenderEvent]
- [x] CHK021 Are API error and authorization scenarios covered by written requirements for invalid scope parameters, unauthorized read access, and invalid or stale render-event submissions? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-001a, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §GET /api/v1/forecast-accuracy, Contract §POST /api/v1/forecast-accuracy/{forecastAccuracyRequestId}/render-events]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are boundary-case requirements defined for buckets where actual demand is zero, making MAPE or percentage-error calculations undefined or unstable? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyMetricResolution, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyAlignedBucket]
- [x] CHK023 Are data and concurrency requirements defined for repeated planner requests over the same scope or duplicate render-event submissions against the same prepared result? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-018, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyRequest, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyRenderEvent]
- [x] CHK024 Are edge cases specified for partially overlapping forecast and actual datasets so excluded buckets are handled consistently and never shifted to force a comparison? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-007, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §ForecastAccuracyComparisonResult]
- [x] CHK025 Are API pagination, payload-size, and filtering boundaries defined for large aligned-bucket responses over longer time ranges or broader scopes? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §GET /api/v1/forecast-accuracy]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 Are UX requirements defined for how metric-unavailable, unavailable, and error states should be communicated so planners can distinguish missing metrics from missing comparison data? [Non-Functional, UX, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Data Model §Performance Visualization State]
- [x] CHK027 Are data retention and auditability requirements specified for request records, metric-resolution outcomes, prepared comparison results, aligned buckets, and render-event logs? [Non-Functional, Data, Gap, Plan §Storage, Data Model §Overview, Data Model §Relationships]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance requirements quantified for interactive read paths and render-event reporting, not just described qualitatively as “quick enough for interactive use”? [Non-Functional, Performance, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK029 Are security requirements specific about role boundaries between planners allowed to view forecast-accuracy data and clients or actors allowed to report render outcomes? [Non-Functional, Security, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-001a, Plan §Constitution Check, Contract §security]
- [x] CHK030 Are availability and operational-safety requirements defined for how forecast-accuracy retrieval behaves when upstream evaluation data is degraded but forecast and actual lineage remain queryable? [Non-Functional, Reliability, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015, Plan §Constitution Check]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are upstream dependencies on retained historical forecast lineage, approved actual-demand lineage, and retained UC-06 evaluation metrics explicitly documented as requirement constraints rather than left implicit? [Dependencies, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constitution Check, Data Model §Reused Shared Entities and Vocabulary]
- [x] CHK032 Is the assumption that earlier use cases already establish visualization conventions and authenticated planner access validated by linked requirements, or should UC-14 state the minimum contract it depends on? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Structure Decision, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK033 Are dependency requirements defined for how the system classifies metric-source mismatches, alignment refusal, and render failures at the observability level? [Dependency, Gap, Plan §Service Direction, Data Model §Request Status, Data Model §Metric Resolution Status]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any conflict between the spec’s promise to show the default analysis view without an initial error state and the plan’s rule that missing forecasts, missing actuals, or empty overlap must immediately return unavailable or error outcomes? [Conflict, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK035 Is “display prediction-versus-actual comparisons and the available forecast accuracy metrics” established clearly enough to define whether charts, tables, or both are mandatory versus optional presentation forms? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios]
- [x] CHK036 Are the terms “historical forecast,” “actual demand,” “comparison bucket,” “prepared result,” “view status,” and “render outcome” used consistently enough across spec, plan, data model, and contract to support one canonical UC-14 vocabulary? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §Canonical UC-14 Vocabulary, Contract §components.schemas]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist is intentionally lightweight and author-oriented, with equal coverage across UX, data, performance, API, and security requirement quality.
